coreIssue: This growth conversation is about whether Ahmed, as Head of Product, can define and step into more strategic ownership instead of defaulting to tactical delivery detail.
openingQuestion: What would next-level Head of Product scope look like in your day-to-day if you were operating with the visibility and influence you want?
listenFor: whether he names one concrete strategic area he wants to own end-to-end rather than listing activities
whether he pivots quickly into delivery detail when you ask about influence, stakeholder trust, or decision authority
if they identify specific stakeholders or forums where their voice carries less weight than the scope they want
avoid: do not turn this into a critique of his current execution habits before you have tested how he defines senior product leadership
do not leave with a broad development list that lacks one strategic ownership bet tied to Head of Product-level influence
goodOutcome: You and Ahmed have agreed one Head of Product-level area he will own more strategically, plus the stakeholder influence expected for that scope.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where Ahmed moved into tactical detail instead of framing strategic direction, and use it to test whether the pattern is pressure or role-definition.
